step_by_step_thinking: I agree with the chosen answer B because filtration primarily serves to remove lower-energy X-rays, thus reducing noise and increasing the signal-to-noise ratio in the resulting radiograph. While other factors like increased scatter can be managed through proper technique rather than inherent properties of filtration materials, the main goal remains to enhance image quality by focusing on useful energy ranges. Therefore, my agreement supports maintaining B as the best answer based on current evidence and practical considerations in radiography. Answer choice: B. Agree.
